ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ (Soups and broths) used as closest defensible category; no specific Moringa pod soup code exists in FoodEx2.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Drumstick Soup is approximately 200 ml (about one medium bowl), which is the typical portion served as a starter or side dish in South Indian households.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for Drumstick Soup are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods using Moringa oleifera pod composition data and standard South Indian soup preparation ratios. No exact entry for prepared Drumstick Soup exists; values represent a best-estimate composite based on the primary ingredient (drumstick pods) and typical recipe proportions. USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy, NDB 11274 — Drumstick pods, raw) was used as a cross-reference for key micronutrients.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — peer-reviewed analogous estimate for high-fibre vegetable soups
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Drumstick (Moringa) Soup. GI estimated at 20 (low) based on analogy with high-fibre, low-carbohydrate vegetable soups and broths documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). The high dietary fibre content (2.5 g/100 g) and low net carbohydrate density (4.5 g/100 g) of Moringa pods strongly support a low GI classification. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (20 × 4.5 × 2) / 100 = 1.8, confirming very low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: High potassium (220 mg/100 g), moderate sodium (150 mg/100 g added from seasoning)
hypertension.guidance: Drumstick Soup provides a favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio that supports blood pressure regulation; individuals with hypertension should request preparation with minimal added salt to keep sodium below 300 mg per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (~20) and low glycemic load (~1.8 per 200 ml serving) due to high dietary fibre and low net carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Drumstick Soup is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; its very low GI and GL minimise postprandial glucose excursions, and Moringa bioactive compounds (isothiocyanates) have been associated with improved insulin sensitivity in preliminary studies.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.2 g/100 g), zero cholesterol, high potassium, and anti-inflammatory Moringa phytonutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Drumstick Soup is a heart-friendly choice due to its negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and potassium content that supports vascular health; the anti-inflammatory properties of Moringa oleifera may provide additional cardioprotective benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory phytonutrients in Moringa oleifera pods
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Moringa pods contain quercetin and other anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce joint inflammation; Drumstick Soup can be included regularly in the diet of individuals managing rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Drumstick Soup, known in Tamil as Murungakkai Soup (முருங்கைக்காய் சூப்) and in Hindi as Sahjan ka Soup (सहजन का सूप), is a traditional South Indian medicinal soup prepared from the pods of the Moringa oleifera tree. The soup is made by boiling drumstick pod pieces with onions, tomatoes, and garlic until tender, then extracting the soft pulp and combining it with the cooking broth. It is seasoned with cumin, black pepper, and turmeric, giving it a mildly spicy, earthy flavour with a thin, slightly fibrous texture. Drumstick Soup is a staple in Tamil Nadu and Kerala households, particularly valued during seasonal transitions for its immunity-boosting properties. Nutritionally, it is low in calories (approximately 35 kcal per 100 g), low in fat (1.2 g), and provides meaningful amounts of vitamin C (45 mg), vitamin A (120 mcg RAE), iron (1.5 mg), calcium (45 mg), potassium (220 mg), and dietary fibre (2.5 g). Its very low glycemic index (~20) and glycemic load (~1.8 per 200 ml serving) make it an excellent choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The high potassium content relative to sodium supports blood pressure regulation, making it suitable for hypertensive individuals when prepared with minimal added salt. Moringa bioactive compounds, including isothiocyanates and quercetin, contribute anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects relevant to cardiovascular and joint health. Drumstick Soup is naturally vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: Is drumstick soup good for diabetes?
What are the health benefits of Murungakkai Soup?
How many calories in drumstick soup?
Is Moringa soup good for blood pressure?
What is Murungakkai Soup made of?
Is drumstick soup good for weight loss?
Can I eat drumstick soup if I have high cholesterol?
What is the GI of drumstick soup?
What vitamins are in drumstick soup?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Moringa pod composition data and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy). No exact entry for prepared Drumstick Soup exists in any approved primary source; values represent a best-estimate composite for a standard South Indian preparation. GI is an analogous estimate based on high-fibre vegetable soup data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero — Drumstick Soup is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally absent. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero for this plant-based soup with no fortification or animal-derived fat. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based preparation. trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero; no hydrogenated fats used. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for prepared Drumstick Soup is not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for the prepared soup form is not reported in available approved sources. 3. Sodium value of 150 mg/100 g reflects a standard home-preparation estimate with moderate salt addition; sodium will vary significantly with recipe. 4. Portions input contained three identical duplicate entries (200 ml, is_default: true); deduplicated to a single canonical serving of 200 g/200 ml. 5. GI of 20 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Drumstick Soup was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2024. 6. Vitamin A reported as 120 mcg RAE, consistent with ICMR-NIN data for Moringa pods (beta-carotene conversion applied). 7. Individuals on warfarin or other anticoagulants should consult a physician before consuming Drumstick Soup regularly due to its vitamin K content (~15 mcg/100 g).
